concerns.text: Task T026 states: '...to control the family-wise error rate per FR-007'. FR-007 allows Bonferroni or Benjamini-Hochberg. However, Benjamini-Hochberg controls the False Discovery Rate (FDR), not the Family-Wise Error Rate (FWER). Bonferroni controls FWER. By explicitly linking BH to FWER control, the task creates a semantic contradiction with the statistical definition required by the spec, risking the implementation of a method that does not satisfy the 'control FWER' constraint if the spec intended strict FWER control, or mislabeling the output if BH is used. The task must clarify the target metric (FDR vs FWER) to match the chosen method.

Task T025 describes implementing a 'Permutation Test on the absolute differences of squared deviations'. FR-005 mandates a test on 'absolute differences of squared deviations'. However, the plan (T025) and task description shift the statistical object to 'variance of |Acc_A - Acc_B|' (mean absolute deviation proxy). This is a semantic drift: 'squared deviations' implies variance of residuals, while 'absolute differences' implies mean absolute deviation. The task must explicitly state it is testing the variance of the squared deviations to preserve the FR-005 constraint.

Task T014 writes raw results to 'data/processed/evaluation_runs.csv'. The Plan (Storage & Schema Alignment) and Spec (Success Criteria) define the Single Source of Truth for raw evaluations as 'results/raw_evaluations.csv'. Writing to 'data/processed' violates the 'Single Source of Truth' principle (Constitution Principle IV) and the defined storage schema, creating a path mismatch that breaks downstream tasks (T018+) expecting files in 'results/'.

Task T005b enforces a strict '100-100k' sample size filter. FR-001 specifies a range of '100 to [deferred]' (open upper bound). While Constitution Principle VII mentions '100 to 100,000', the Spec's FR-001 uses '[deferred]' to allow for larger datasets if justified. Hardcoding a 100k cap in the task without an explicit exception clause for justified larger datasets constitutes a silent narrowing of the FR-001 scope, potentially excluding valid large-scale datasets mentioned in the Assumptions section.
